rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes several logical leaps: 48-participant university-pool study → 'foundational step toward truly mixed-initiative search interfaces'; black-box model treatment → full interface contribution claim; small Likert delta (0.6 pts) and 22% reformulation reduction treated as conclusive rather than preliminary. Checking logical consistency between premises and generalisation scope is central to any reading of 'check this' on an academic excerpt.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative claims (22% fewer reformulations, 0.6-point Likert improvement) are stated without confidence intervals, effect-size context, or significance values. The 'first empirical demonstration' framing requires those numbers to carry the weight of novelty; verifying that reported evidence actually supports each claim is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.4: The excerpt makes at least two 'first' priority claims ('first interface to embed…', 'first HCI study to measure…'). Assessing whether the cited TREC-style prior-work landscape actually leaves these gaps open is central to any substantive check of this artifact.
